note: LLM-generation-latency ratchet (tempdoc 640 L) — the inference-runtime sibling of perf-ratchet-baselines.v1.json. Gates TTFT + end-to-end summarization p50 (medians from `jseval llm-bench`, which already does warmup-discard + multi-sample) against a RELATIVE ratio band via `jseval llm-gate` (llm_gate.evaluate -> diff_gate.compare_ratio). NO absolute SLO. Per MACHINE/config, NOT per corpus (the bench is corpus-agnostic). `metrics` is PROJECTED from a green bench via `jseval llm-gate --update-baseline` (measured, never hand-typed) — empty here = un-pinned (the gate SKIPs) until a baseline bench is pinned on this machine. tokens/sec (token_rate_median_tps, higher-is-better) is now CAPTURED (640 D — NO backend change): the chat done-event emits promptTokens+totalTokens flat (ConversationEngine:357-361), so llm_bench derives completion = total − prompt; live-confirmed (25.5 t/s). It is a 3rd llm-gen metric; `metrics` below pins TTFT+e2e and adds tokens/sec on the next `--update-baseline` re-pin (a bench run where doc-discovery serves the eval index).
